APPENDIX N —INDOOR TRADE SHOWS AND EXHIBITIONS

SBC 801-CC-2024 
1995

APPENDIX N—Indoor Trade Shows and Exhibitions 
The provisions contained in this appendix are not mandatory unless specifically referenced in the 
adopting ordinance.

❖ General Comments

This appendix is intended to address hazards 
associated with larger, more complex trade 
shows and exhibitions. Although many of these 
requirements are already included in various 
locations in the code, some of the more 
important items, such as requirements for 
covered booths and multistory booths are not 
in the existing code. In addition, having the 
requirements covering these events in a single 
location, with pointers to other locations within 
the code, makes it easier for those organizing 
exhibitions and individual exhibitors who are 
unfamiliar with the entire fire code to locate the 
requirements that are applicable to them.

❖ Purpose

The purpose of this appendix is to provide a 
structured set of requirements specifically for 
indoor trade shows and exhibitions. These 
requirements are already basic requirements 
contained in the code. This appendix focuses 
on the issues that typically are present in indoor 
trade shows. 
N101 GENERAL 
N101.1 Scope. Indoor trade shows and exhibitions 
with temporary vendor displays or booths within 
any indoor occupancy classification shall be in 
accordance with this appendix and all other 
applicable requirements of this code. 
Compliance with this appendix is not required 
where Section N101.1.1 or N101.1.2 is applicable.

❖ The minimum square foot thresholds clarify 
that the appendix does not cover small trade 
shows, like those with traditional tabletop 
displays and small booths. It only covers the 
larger exhibitions, and those with covered and 
multilevel booths, which pose additional safety 
concerns. 
N101.1.1 
Nonsprinklered buildings. In a 
building that is not equipped throughout with an 
automatic sprinkler system, the aggregate exhibit 
area must be less than 140 m2 of floor area and meet 
both of the following conditions: 
1. The exhibit area does not include any 
covered or multiple-level exhibits or booths. 
2. Not fewer than two remote exits or exit 
access doors in compliance with Chapter 10 
are provided.

❖ This section states the maximum square 
footage and conditions that will obviate the 
need for compliance with the provisions of this 
appendix in nonsprinklered buildings. 
N101.1.2 
Sprinklered 
buildings. 
In 
a 
building that is equipped throughout with an 
automatic sprinkler system with a minimum design 
density of ordinary hazard Group 1, the aggregate 
exhibit area must be less than 420 m2 of floor area 
and meet both of the following conditions: 
1. The exhibit area does not include any 
covered or multiple-level exhibits or booths. 
2. Not fewer than two remote exits or exit 
access doors in compliance with Chapter 10 
are provided.

❖ This section states the maximum square 
footage and conditions that will obviate the 
need for compliance with the provisions of this 
appendix in sprinklered buildings. 
N101.2 Permit required. An operational permit 
for trade shows and exhibitions shall be required as 
set forth in Section 105.6.14.

chunks.chunk_id: SBC 801 - The Saudi Fire Protection Code (3)-1801-2061_sec710_12_59e08133
chunks.code_id: SBC 801 - The Saudi Fire Protection Code (3)-1801-2061
chunks.content:
❖ The regulation of indoor trade shows is based 
on the requirements for operational permits as 
outlined in Section 105.6.13. 
N101.3 Application. A permit application for a 
trade show or exhibition shall be submitted to the 
fire official prior to the start of the event in a time 
frame 
established 
by 
the 
jurisdiction. 
The 
application shall include documentation that 
identifies all of the following: 
1. The means of egress. 
2. The locations and widths of exits and aisles. 
3. The locations of exit signs. 
4. The total square meters of spaces. 
5. The location and arrangement of all booths 
and cooking equipment. 
6. The location of all fire protection equipment. 
-

APPENDIX N —INDOOR TRADE SHOWS AND EXHIBITIONS

SBC 801-CC-2024 
1996

7. The type and location of any heating and 
electrical equipment, where applicable. 
8. The location of any covered or multiple-level 
booths. 
9. Construction documents for any covered or 
multiple-level booths. 
10. The storage locations and quantities of any 
highly combustible goods. 
11. The location and type of any vehicle 
displays, where applicable.

❖ This section defines the information needed to 
obtain a permit for one of these larger 
exhibitions. Having this information in writing 
makes it easier for all parties to understand the 
information needed to gain approval for an 
exhibition. 
N102 DEFINITIONS 
N102.1 Definitions. For the purpose of this 
appendix, certain terms are defined as follows: 
COOKING. Heating food products to a temperature 
of 63°C or higher by baking, braising, boiling, 
frying or grilling. 
COVERED BOOTH. An exhibit that has an 
obstruction placed over the exhibit above floor level 
that resembles a roof, canopy, tent or other 
obstruction, other than vertical signs or banners. 
MULTIPLE-LEVEL BOOTH. An exhibit that has 
a second level or tier constructed on top of the 
exhibit or portion of the exhibit that is accessible to 
the public, or includes a live load above the exhibit 
area floor level.

❖ Definitions 
of 
terms 
can 
help 
in 
the 
understanding and application of the code 
requirements. This section provides the code 
user information for the proper application of 
the indicated terms used in this appendix. 
Terms also may be defined in Chapter 2 of 
SBC 801, in another Saudi Codes as indicated 
in Section 201.3 of SBC 801, or the dictionary 
meaning may be all that is needed

Certain requirements in this code are based on 
code provisions in SBC 201, SBC 501 and SBC 
701. A review of definitions included in those 
codes will aid in the understanding of many of 
the requirements contained in this code. 
N103 PUBLIC SAFETY FOR EVENTS 
N103.1 Fire safety and evacuation plan. A fire 
safety and evacuation plan shall be provided in 
accordance with Section 404.2.  
Exception: Where the Fire official determines that 
the nature of the exhibition, display or the activities 
therein does not pose an increased hazard to public 
safety.

❖ This section simply provides a convenient 
pointer to public safety related requirements in 
Chapter 4 regarding a fire safety and 
evacuation plan. The exception provides some 
flexibility to the fire official where such plans are 
not necessary. 
N103.2 Fire watch personnel. Where, in the 
opinion of the fire official, it is essential for public 
safety in a trade show or exhibition, either because 
of the number or persons present or because of the 
nature of the performance, exhibition, display or 
activity, the owner or owner’s authorized agent 
shall provide one or more fire watch personnel in 
accordance with Section 403.11.1.

chunks.chunk_id: SBC 801 - The Saudi Fire Protection Code (3)-1801-2061_sec710_13_8e9356a3
chunks.code_id: SBC 801 - The Saudi Fire Protection Code (3)-1801-2061
chunks.content:
❖ The requirement for fire watch personnel is 
determined by the fire official. The section 
offers no further thresholds for triggering this 
requirement. Some things to possibly consider 
are: 
• Level of security for the exhibit during hours 
that the exhibit is closed. 
• The types of materials used in the exhibit. 
• The size of the exhibit, and range of visibility 
throughout the exhibit. 
• The amount of hidden areas around the 
exhibit. 
• The potential fire load present. 
• Whether the building is sprinklered. 
• The type of construction of the host building. 
N103.3 Crowd managers. Where events involve a 
gathering of more than 1,000 people, trained crowd 
managers shall be provided in accordance with 
Section 403.12.3.

❖ This section is similar to the requirement for 
standby personnel for tents in Section 
3107.17.2. Also note that Section 3106.4.3 
addresses outdoor assembly events and crowd 
managers are required where the events 
exceed 1,000 people. There is an exception for 
outdoor gatherings under 1,000 people in 
Section 403.12.3.1, which is consistent with 
this section. Large assemblies of people create 
the need for crowd management due to the 
increased potential for panic and fear in 
emergency situations. Crowd managers can be 
personnel already assigned and employed by 
the facility, provided that they are trained as 
crowd managers to fulfill this requirement. At 
the time of an emergency, the trained crowd 
-

APPENDIX N —INDOOR TRADE SHOWS AND EXHIBITIONS

SBC 801-CC-2024 
1997

managers 
would 
take 
on 
additional 
responsibilities to control and direct the 
audience or attendees in a safe manner. 
N104 INTERIOR FINISH AND 
DECORATIVE MATERIALS 
N104.1 General. Interior finish, interior trim, 
furniture, furnishings and decorative materials, 
including decorative vegetation, used in exhibition 
areas shall comply with the requirements of this 
section and Chapter 8.

❖ This section references Chapter 8 for interior 
finish 
requirements. 
In 
addition, 
the 
requirements of N104.2 relating to the 
presence of sprinklers also apply for interior 
finish materials in exhibits. Without sprinklers, 
Class A finish materials are required. This 
requirement addresses the problems that 
exhibitions and trade shows can create with 
substantial amounts of combustible materials 
used in booths and displays. 
N104.2 Interior wall and ceiling finish. The 
materials used for interior wall and ceiling finish of 
exhibit booths and displays in exhibition areas shall 
comply with one of the following: 
1. Where the building is not equipped 
throughout with an automatic sprinkler 
system in accordance with Section 903.3.1.1, 
the wall and ceiling finish materials are 
required to be Class A in accordance with 
Section 803. 
2. Where the building is equipped throughout 
with an automatic sprinkler system in 
accordance with Section 903.3.1.1, the wall 
and ceiling finish materials are required to be 
not less than Class B in accordance with 
Section 803.

❖ This section includes a set of interior finish 
requirements that are applicable to an event in 
a Group A occupancy where there is a 
significant amount of combustible material in 
the exhibits and booth themselves that was not 
present in the building when it was initially 
designed. Due to the potential fuel loading in 
the exhibition area, interior wall and ceiling 
finish materials are required to meet Class A in 
buildings without a sprinkler system and Class 
B in buildings with a sprinkler system. 
N105 MULTIPLE-LEVEL BOOTHS 
N105.1 Construction documents. Construction 
documents for all multiple-level booths shall be 
stamped by a registered design professional and 
shall be submitted with the permit application to the 
fire official or the fire official, as appropriate.

chunks.chunk_id: SBC 801 - The Saudi Fire Protection Code (3)-1801-2061_sec710_14_20cf713f
chunks.code_id: SBC 801 - The Saudi Fire Protection Code (3)-1801-2061
chunks.content:
❖ Multiple-level 
booths 
require 
additional 
attention for several reasons: 
1. Structural loads on the supporting structure 
from higher dead loads and people standing 
on the upper levels. 
2. Additional challenges egressing from the 
booths, especially from upper levels. 
3. Additional challenges for fire protection with 
sprinklers. 
4. Additional challenges for notifying people of 
a fire or other emergency to people within the 
booth. 
Therefore, specific construction documents are 
required to detail the booth and provide adequate 
structural support and fire protection are necessary. 
N105.2 Structural design. Multiple-level booths 
shall be designed and constructed in accordance 
with Chapter 16 of SBC 201.

❖ Structural safety is an issue in multilevel booths 
because of their size, and because a live load 
from occupant traffic on the upper levels must 
be accounted for. 
N105.3 Means of egress. Upper levels of multiple-
level booths with an occupant load greater than 10 
persons shall have not fewer than two exits or exit 
access that are separated in accordance with Section 
1007.1.1.

❖ The concern for means of egress in a multilevel 
booth is egress from an upper level. Exits and 
exit access with the same integrity as for any 
building are necessary. This would include 
stairways that meet the requirements in 
Chapter 10 for stairways. 
N105.4 Automatic 
sprinkler 
systems. 
An 
approved automatic sprinkler system in accordance 
with Section 903.3.1.1 shall be provided in 
multiple-level booths exceeding 37.2 m2 in floor 
area per level.

❖ Both multilevel booths and covered booths can 
block sprinkler protection provided in exhibition 
areas, and also can include a significant 
amount of fuel loading, which justifies the 
requirement for temporary automatic sprinklers 
for the larger booths. 
N105.5 Inspection. Inspection to verify that 
multiple-level booths are constructed in accordance 
with the construction documents and structural 
-

chunks.chunk_id: SBC 801 - The Saudi Fire Protection Code (3)-1801-2061_sec314_0_86e465d6
chunks.code_id: SBC 801 - The Saudi Fire Protection Code (3)-1801-2061
chunks.content:
APPENDIX N —INDOOR TRADE SHOWS AND EXHIBITIONS

SBC 801-CC-2024 
1998

design details required by this section shall be 
approved by the building official.

❖ Inspections are required primarily to ensure the 
structural integrity of the multilevel booth. 
N105.6 Fire alarm and detection. Each multiple-
level booth with a floor area exceeding 11 m2 on 
any level shall be provided with an approved fire 
alarm system in accordance with Section 907.2.

❖ Additional fire alarm protection is necessary to 
address the additional challenges of notifying 
occupants within larger booths. 
N106 COVERED BOOTHS 
N106.1 Automatic 
sprinkler 
systems. 
An 
approved automatic sprinkler system in accordance 
with Section 903.3.1.1 shall be provided in covered 
booths exceeding 10 m2 in floor area per level.

❖ Both multilevel booths and covered booths can 
block sprinkler protection provided in exhibition 
areas, and also can include a significant 
amount of fuel loading,which justifies the 
requirement for temporary automatic sprinklers 
for the larger booths. 
N106.2 Fire alarm and detection. Each covered 
booth with a floor area exceeding 11 m2 on any 
level shall be provided with an approved fire alarm 
system in accordance with Section 907.2.

❖ Additional fire alarm protection is necessary to 
address the additional challenges of notifying 
occupants within larger booths, which begin to 
become buildings within buildings. 
N107 DISPLAY AND STORAGE OF 
HAZARDOUS AND COMBUSTIBLE 
MATERIALS 
N107.1 Hazardous materials. The display of 
hazardous materials shall comply with Section 314 
and Chapters 50 through 67. The storage of 
hazardous materials in indoor trade shows and 
exhibition areas shall be prohibited.

❖ This section points to restrictions on hazardous 
materials commonly found in trade shows as 
discussed in Section 314. 
N107.1.1 
Display near exit. The display of 
hazardous materials within 1500 mm of an exit shall 
be prohibited.

❖ Also consistent with Section 314 is a restriction 
on the proximity of displays of hazardous 
materials to exits, to reduce the risk of 
impairing egress from the exhibition. 
N107.2 Storage 
of 
combustible 
materials. 
Storage of combustible materials shall comply with 
Section 315.

❖ This section covers basic requirements for 
display and storage in exhibitions by reference 
to Section 315, which addresses general 
storage in buildings. 
N107.3 Vehicles. The display of liquid- or gas-
fueled vehicles, boats or other motor craft in indoor 
trade shows and exhibition areas shall comply with 
Sections 314.4 and N107.3.1 through N107.3.3.

❖ The use of vehicles and other fueled 
equipment is popular in trade shows and 
exhibitions. 
Sections 
N107.3.1 
through 
N107.3.5 address the common problems 
associated with vehicles inside a building, 
requiring 
that: 
batteries 
be 
rendered 
inoperable; vehicle fueling be restricted by 
location 
and 
amount; 
fuel 
systems 
be 
inspected; tank openings be locked and 
sealed; vehicles not obstruct egress; and 
compressed gas, LP-gas or hydrogen be 
limited 
in 
dualfuel 
vehicles 
to 
prevent 
accidental engine starts. 
N107.3.1 
Batteries in vehicles. Vehicle 
batteries shall be rendered inoperable. Batteries in 
liquid- 
and 
gas-fueled 
vehicles 
shall 
be 
disconnected. Batteries in electric vehicles shall be 
rendered inoperable by the removal of fuses or other 
approved methods but shall not be required to be 
disconnected.

❖ See the commentary to Section N107.3. 
N107.3.2 
Vehicle fuel. Vehicle fuel shall 
comply 
with 
Sections 
N107.3.2.1 
through 
N107.3.2.4.

❖ See the commentary to Section N107.3. 
N107.3.2.1 
Fueling within the structure. 
Vehicles shall not be fueled or defueled within the 
structure.

❖ See the commentary to Section N107.3. 
N107.3.2.2 
Vehicle fuel tanks. Vehicle fuel 
tanks shall contain not more than one quarter of the 
tank capacity or 19 L of fuel, whichever is less.

chunks.chunk_id: SBC 801 - The Saudi Fire Protection Code (3)-1801-2061_sec314_1_8dcfe223
chunks.code_id: SBC 801 - The Saudi Fire Protection Code (3)-1801-2061
chunks.content:
❖ See the commentary to Section N107.3. 
N107.3.2.3 
Vehicle fuel systems. Vehicle fuel 
systems shall be inspected for leaks prior to the 
vehicle being brought into the structure.

❖ See the commentary to Section N107.3. 
-

APPENDIX N —INDOOR TRADE SHOWS AND EXHIBITIONS

SBC 801-CC-2024 
1999

N107.3.2.4 
Vehicle 
fuel 
tank 
openings. 
Vehicle fuel tank openings shall be locked and 
sealed to prevent the escape of vapors.

❖ See the commentary to Section N107.3. 
N107.3.3 
Obstruction by vehicles. Vehicles 
shall not be located in such a manner that they 
obstruct a means of egress.

❖ See the commentary to Section N107.3. 
N107.3.4 
Gas-powered 
vehicles. 
Compressed 
natural 
gas 
(CNG), 
liquefied 
petroleum gas (LPG) 
or hydrogen-powered 
vehicles present in indoor trade shows and 
exhibition areas shall comply with Sections 
N107.3.4.1 through N107.3.4.3.

❖ See the commentary to Section N107.3. 
N107.3.4.1 
Shutoff valves. Shutoff valves 
shall be closed and the engine shall be operated 
until it stops. Valves shall remain closed until the 
vehicle is removed.

❖ See the commentary to Section N107.3. 
N107.3.4.2 
Battery hot lead. The hot lead of 
the battery shall be disconnected.

❖ See the commentary to Section N107.3. 
N107.3.4.3 
Dual-fuel vehicles equipped to 
operate on gasoline. Dual-fuel vehicles equipped 
to operate on gasoline as well as on CNG, LPG or 
hydrogen shall comply with Section 3107.15.

❖ See the commentary to Section N107.3. 
N107.3.5 
Competitions or demonstrations. 
Competitions or demonstrations using any type of 
vehicle shall comply with Section 3107.15.5.

❖ See the commentary to Section N107.3. 
N107.4 Fueled equipment other than vehicles. 
Fueled equipment other than vehicles shall comply 
with Section 313.

❖ See the commentary to Section 313. 
N107.5 LP-gas containers Liquefied petroleum 
(LP) gas containers shall comply with Sections 
N107.5.1 through N107.5.5 and Chapter 61.

❖ LP-gas containers present a risk in any 
building. The containers must be stored 
properly to prevent any such container from 
turning into a missile and injuring people. In this 
section, the amount and size of LP-gas 
containers are restricted to minimize the risk of 
injury or the risk of fire. 
N107.5.1 
LP-gas containers exceeding 5 kg 
of water capacity. The use of LP-gas containers 
exceeding 5 kg of water capacity shall be 
prohibited.

❖ See the commentary to Section N107.5. 
N107.5.2 
Where more than one LP-gas 
container is present in the same area. Where 
more than one LP-gas container is present in the 
same area, cylinders shall be separated from each 
other by a minimum of 6 m.

❖ See the commentary to Section N107.5. 
N107.5.3 
Equipment 
for 
LP-gas 
containers. Equipment for LP-gas containers, 
including tanks, piping, hoses, fittings, valves, 
tubing and other related components, shall be 
approved and shall comply with Chapter 61 and 
with the applicable requirements of SBC 1201.

❖ See the commentary to Section N107.5. 
N107.5.4 
Securing of LP-gas containers. 
Portable LP-gas containers shall be securely 
fastened 
in 
place 
to 
prevent 
unauthorized 
movement.

❖ See the commentary to Section N107.5. 
N107.5.5 
Spare LP-gas containers. Spare 
LP-gas containers not connected to an approved 
appliance shall be stored in a location and manner 
approved by the fire official.

chunks.chunk_id: SBC 801 - The Saudi Fire Protection Code (3)-1801-2061_sec314_2_1ef86043
chunks.code_id: SBC 801 - The Saudi Fire Protection Code (3)-1801-2061
chunks.content:
❖ See the commentary to Section N107.5. 
N107.6 Cooking and open-flame devices. All 
cooking equipment and any open-flame devices 
shall comply with the requirements of Section 308 
of this code and with Chapter 5 of SBC 501. 
Cooking equipment shall be separated from 
combustible material display or storage by a 
horizontal distance of not less than 1.5 m.

❖ A typical activity during these indoor trade 
shows is cooking—either for demonstration, or 
for food service to attendees. This section 
simply provides a pointer to applicable sections 
of the code for requirements. 
N108 MEANS OF EGRESS 
N108.1 Means of egress from the indoor trade 
show or exhibition area. Means of egress from the 
indoor trade show or exhibition area shall comply 
with Chapter 10 and with Sections N108.2 and 
N108.3.

❖ Section N108 references Chapter 10 means of 
egress requirements, and also restricts storage 
in aisles and corridors of exhibitions where the 
general public is present. The layout of a large 
exhibition needs to be carefully planned to 
-

APPENDIX N —INDOOR TRADE SHOWS AND EXHIBITIONS

SBC 801-CC-2024 
2000

avoid dead ends, areas of crowd congestion, 
excessive travel distances, and poor visibility of 
means of egress. In other words, the layout 
must meet the requirements for means of 
egress given in Chapter 10 for all other parts of 
a building. 
N108.2 Design of means of egress. The design of 
means of egress shall take into consideration the 
exhibit layout and the anticipated crowd movement 
during the event.

❖ See the commentary to Section N108.1. 
N108.3 Aisles and corridors. Aisles and corridors 
within the exhibit area shall be kept free from 
obstructions when the public is present. Storage of 
any kind in aisles or corridors within the exhibit 
area is not permitted.

❖ See the commentary to Section N108.1. 
N109 REFERENCED STANDARDS 
N109.1 General. See Table N109.1 for standards 
that are referenced in various sections of this 
appendix. Standards are listed by the standard 
identification with the effective date, standard title, 
and the section or sections of this appendix that 
reference the standard.

-
